title: Generating an ATF
wikitext:
[]: Generating an ATF.             Quote Post by evilhead008 » Mon Feb 18, 2008 9:58 pm Hello... Now, I was just re-watching Asuka/Shinji/Rei's battle vs Sahaquiel, and this thought just sprang up... Shinji: Here we go! Shinji: Start! -------------- Shigeru : Distance 12,000. -------------- Shinji: Field to full power! Shinji: Uhh. Rei: Unit Two, increase Field to full power. Asuka: Increasing! Shinji: Right now! Asuka: This...!!

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Jul 23, 2008 12:31 pm Great question. I would assume that since the evas are controlled by thought, like thinking walk and it walks, that thinking ATF would bring the ATF. This might work out after the ATF is initially started, with having to think of increasing it or spreading it to do so. However, the notion of turning an ATF on alludes me, so....I will thus leave it to further speculation to whomever posts next.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 23, 2008 12:43 pm Their AT fields were already deployed, they were merely "pushing it to the limit". Another wonderfully fuzzy area in the science of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 23, 2008 6:09 pm I would imagine it as some sort of conscious act of willpower in resisting or defying others... generally speaking, maintaining a "Bring it!" frame of mind. Then again, the pilots could be thinking "projected tessellation of regular hexagons in orange light..." over and over again for all we know.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jul 24, 2008 1:03 pm Expecto Patronum ?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jul 24, 2008 4:44 pm LOLz, if only it were that easy, but Evangelion>Harry Potter.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Jul 24, 2008 4:48 pm Yeah, but then again Everything>Harry Pothead. almost

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jul 24, 2008 4:58 pm oooo, tough call.....

[]: Re: Generating an ATF.             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Thu Jul 24, 2008 5:53 pm evilhead008 wrote: So I was wondering, what does a pilot have to think in order to generate an ATF?

[]: ??             Quote Post by IX » Thu Jul 31, 2008 4:31 pm well if you stop to think it is clear like it is said before its thought, but may be an active action be the eva and a thought of the pilots part the expound, Shinji thinks about the A. T. Field and his mom, or the eva unit 01 creates it using the Bio half of its being and ends in the light of ones soul or both the evas and the pilots, expanding to a "physical" sheild

[]:             Quote Post by IX » Sun Aug 03, 2008 2:41 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: Expecto Patronum ?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:22 pm Don't the Evas generate and AT Field on their own without the pilots? ATF = light of the soul and Evas have souls and all. So what do the pilots do to manipulate it? Provide another soul to strengthen it I guess?

[]:             Quote Post by IX » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:53 pm its clear that the pilot can activate the ATF using the mind, but to both extreams, their has been times where the pilots were cut off from doing just that

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:56 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Don't the Evas generate and AT Field on their own without the pilots? ATF = light of the soul and Evas have souls and all.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:59 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: but you have to keep those nasty power supressors in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by IX » Sun Aug 03, 2008 6:03 pm just to end after reading all the response the AT Field is something that we can debate for (insert stupidly long amount of time here), but it is just, at the end of the day a warped mind came up with, or a clear turned warped by threats of the 3rd kind (insert skull&cross bones here)

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Aug 03, 2008 6:54 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: And the pilot really acts as the conscious half in order to get past that suppressant and 'complete' the ATF... That makes sense.
